data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 7/2/26?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) opened higher on Thursday but have since reversed course, falling into negative territory.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The culprit behind the reversal is June's jobs report. During the month, the U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs, well below the consensus estimate of 110,000 additions. Making matters worse, April's and May's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 74,000 jobs.

Furthermore, the unemployment rate fell to a one-year low of 4.2% during the month. That appears to be positive on the surface. However, the fall was attributed to the labor force participation rate dropping to 61.5%. This indicates a smaller share of people were working or looking for work.

AI Sector Weakens as Investors Shift Into Software and Financials

Meanwhile, memory and semiconductor stocks continue to face pressure. Micron (MU), AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), and Lam Research (LRCX) are all down by over 5% at the time of writing. In recent days, investors have aggressively realized profits from AI-beneficiaries following significant year-to-date gains.

Some of that capital has rotated into beaten-down software and financial stocks. These include Palantir (PLTR), Adobe (ADBE), Visa (V), and Mastercard (MA).
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "81 Million Login Attempts": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Gains Following Huge Brute Force Attack
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) is no stranger to cybersecurity issues. With so many platforms that people use every day, protecting those platforms from unwanted intrusion is basically second nature. And Microsoft managed to fend off one doozy of an attack recently, as a "password-spraying campaign" fired up only recently. The attack, and subsequent fact that little damage was done, was welcome news to shareholders, who gave Microsoft shares nearly a 2% boost in Thursday afternoon's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The attack targeted Microsoft 365, reports noted, and featured 81 million login attempts spread out over a period of two weeks. The person behind the threat tried using command-line interface (CLI) systems with still-valid username and password combinations that were found from past system breaches. By then, quite a few of those had likely been changed, but some were not, and that opened up an opportunity for the hacker to use the Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC) O Auth system.

That in turn allowed the hacker to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) in at least some cases. And with that came some key realizations about MFA in cybersecurity, and some of its key limitations. In some cases, reports noted, MFA had been applied, but only to certain applications as opposed to all cloud apps. MFA also may not have been enforced for some user groups, like regular end users, as opposed to administrators.

The Disc-to-Digital Conundrum

This generation—and to a limited extent the last generation—has given rise to a particular problem for gamers. When you buy a game on disc, why do you not have the ability to download the game from the Xbox store? This is particularly important if the disc should be unplayable due to scratches or the like. But Microsoft is currently testing a tool that may help on that front, albeit too late for a lot of gamers who already bought digital copies of games they already owned on disc.

The feature is currently known as "Disc2Digital," reports note, and that may well change by the time it goes live. Reports also note that it will work for Xbox One and Xbox Series X discs only, so those hoping to digitize their old Xbox and Xbox 360 titles will be, sadly, out of luck.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 35 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 22.97% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $562.10 per share implies 43.53% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Plunges as Semi Has its First Fatal Accident
data.description:
It was likely only a matter of time, but the Tesla Semi from electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) was involved in its first-ever fatal accident recently. The accident left two people dead and a legion of questions in its wake. It also left some very unhappy shareholders, who abandoned their positions and dealt Tesla more than an 8% loss in its share price in Thursday afternoon's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

200% short exposure to TSLA with TSLQ

The accident happened on U.S. 50 in Nevada, reports noted, and the driver of the Tesla Semi apparently fell asleep before driving into two passenger vehicles that had stopped at a red light. Two of the occupants of the passenger vehicles were killed at the scene, and a third person had been flown to a local hospital with "life-threatening injuries."

Tesla Semi trucks do not come with Full Self-Driving (Supervised) capability, reports note. That capability is actually still in testing phases, and a Tesla Semi with FSD test hardware was recently spotted out in California. But there are safety systems that could have stepped in, like automatic emergency braking (AEB) that detect vehicles and other obstacles and brake automatically. Most modern semis have AEB installed, and some new laws are in the works that require them universally. But Tesla has never published safety specs about the Semi, reports note, so we do not know if AEB is in place therein.

Smile! You're on Cabin Camera!

Meanwhile, Tesla drivers may have a new hoop to jump through to use FSD: the cabin camera. The FSD system already uses the cabin camera to confirm that drivers are, indeed, supervising the system. But new reports suggest the cabin camera may go even farther, putting FSD behind a "biometric lock."

A recent deep dive into the Tesla mobile app version 4.58.5 suggests that Tesla wants to put the cabin camera to use in such fashion. There are specific entries in the code relating to "identity check failed" messages and dialog. This actually dovetails well with earlier software updates suggesting that the Tesla cabin camera might start checking driver age with facial analysis. It is not a far cry to go from "checking your age" to "checking to make sure you are this person."

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 11 Buys, 15 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.87% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $404.86 per share implies 3.67% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: S&P 500 Faces Risk of a 'Three-Wave Correction,' Warns Bank of America
data.description:
Paul Ciana, Bank of America's Global Head of Technical Strategy, believes that the S&P 500 (SPX) could face a "three-wave correction" in the coming months. A three-wave correction is a decline that occurs in three stages before the market stabilizes. The correction could bring the index to as low as 6,850, implying downside of 8.5% from current levels.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

"Be cautious if a marginal new high toward ~7,741 occurs as it may be a 'bull trap' consistent with an expanding flat," wrote Ciana. A bull trap refers to a false breakout that reverses lower, while an expanding flat is a technical pattern where price swings get bigger over time instead of tightening.

Ciana Warns of Stretched Positioning as BofA Maintains Bearish Outlook

Ciana believes that price action appears "stretched," while optimism following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension is fading. In addition, Ciana notes that investors should take a defensive stance from July through September.

Earlier this week, Bank of America reiterated its 2026 S&P 500 price target of 7,100. The target implies downside of 5% and is the lowest forecast on Wall Street. The firm cited cooling liquidity drivers, including lower buybacks, dovish central banks, and falling institutional demand.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rallies as Payrolls Miss Chips Into Rate Hike Odds
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Thursday and secured a new intraday all-time high of 52,805.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The gains come despite a significant nonfarm payrolls miss. In June, the U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs, well below the consensus estimate of 110,000. Additionally, payrolls for May and April were revised lower by a combined 74,000 jobs. Still, the labor market is still faring better this year than last year. The economy has added an average of 92,000 jobs per month in 2026, up from 10,000 in 2025.

Furthermore, the unemployment rate slipped to 4.2% from 4.3% in May. That marks the lowest level since June 2025 and was below expectations of 4.3%. However, it comes with a caveat, as the labor force participation rate fell to 0.3% to 61.5%. That decline likely contributed to the lower unemployment rate by reducing the number of people counted as actively looking for work.

The data helped ease rate hike odds, as a weaker labor market discourages higher rates. At the Fed's next meeting on July 29, the odds of a 25 bps hike dropped to 17.6% from 28.9% a day ago. The odds of the same scenario at the September 16 meeting dipped to 45.9% from 49.8%.

"Bottom line: The June jobs report is great for the Federal Reserve, but it's disappointing for the rest of America," said Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long.

Elsewhere, Brent crude (BZ), the global benchmark for oil, is trading slightly lower. Crude has plummeted 38% since hitting a peak in early May, easing inflation concerns.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.65% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: CoreWeave and Nebius Face a Key AI Compute Question: Can the Returns Last?
data.description:
Neocloud providers CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius (NBIS) are both seeing strong demand for AI computing power. However, BNP Paribas says that investors are thinking about each company in different ways. The main question is whether newer cloud providers can continue to generate attractive returns as they quickly add more GPUs to meet AI demand. However, BNP analyst Stefan Slowinski says that prices still look strong for now.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

High conviction CRWV bulls now have this Tradr ETF

He points to recent AI deals by SpaceX (SPCX) with Anthropic and Google (GOOGL), which he estimates could bring in about $30 billion to $50 billion per gigawatt. This suggests that companies that can deliver computing power quickly can still charge high prices. At the same time, BNP believes that the market is starting to change. In fact, companies are now focusing on using AI more efficiently in order to keep costs down, which is a change from before, when they were just trying to use as many AI tokens as possible.

Interestingly, this shift could actually help Nebius. Its AI cloud platform may appeal to businesses that are looking for cheaper options than the expensive models from companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. Nebius could also support open-source AI models that companies can customize, which can lower costs. However, BNP says this advantage has not yet clearly shown up in Nebius's financial results.

CoreWeave Offers a Different Opportunity

CoreWeave, on the other hand, offers a different opportunity. Its stock has not performed as well as Nebius so far, but Slowinski believes that it could catch up if the company executes well in the second half of 2026. Right now, CoreWeave is building a lot of new capacity, which can make its profits look uneven in the short term.

Still, its newer contracts are designed to handle rising costs while keeping profit margins in the mid-20% range. CoreWeave is also further ahead in building its infrastructure, with about 1 gigawatt of active power compared to around 200 megawatts for Nebius.

Because CoreWeave is more developed, BNP expects its profit swings to become smaller over time as new projects make up a smaller part of its overall business. Moreover, Slowinski says that these newer cloud companies may accept lower basic returns than larger cloud providers, since using debt can still help them earn good returns on projects.

Which Neocloud Stock Is the Better Buy?

Overall, out of the two neocloud stocks mentioned above, analysts think that CRWV stock has more room to run than NBIS. In fact, CRWV's price target of $132.35 per share implies 63.1% upside versus NBIS' 12.6%. (See CRWV's Stock Forecast).
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 7/2/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Thursday as investors digest mixed jobs data.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The U.S. economy added 57,000 nonfarm payrolls last month, falling short of the estimate of 110,000. Furthermore, April's and May's payrolls were revised down by a combined 74,000 jobs. At the same time, the unemployment rate eased to 4.2% from 4.3% in May. That was below expectations of 4.3% and also marked a one-year low.

Payrolls Miss Fuels Interest Rate Relief

The nonfarm payrolls miss reduces pressure on the Fed to hike rates. At the next Fed meeting on July 29, the odds of a 25 bps hike dropped to 19.8% from 28.9% a day ago. Lower rates are generally a positive for stocks because they reduce borrowing costs and increase the present value of future earnings. They also encourage investment and growth.

Elsewhere, investors continue to rotate into beaten-down software names. Palantir (PLTR), Fair Isaac (FICO), and Adobe (ADBE) are all well in the green. Furthermore, AI-related stocks are showing mixed price action, highlighted by Intel (INTC), KLA Corp (KLAC), and SanDisk (SNDK).
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: IREN Stock Sinks despite Tapping Oracle and Google Execs in C-Suite Roles
data.description:
Shares in IREN Limited (IREN) tumbled about 9% on Thursday morning despite announcing the appointment of former executives at Oracle (ORCL) and Google (GOOGL) for leadership positions. IREN stock started to plunge on Wednesday following news that Meta (META) plans to enter the AI cloud infrastructure business.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

Explore CWVX for 2X leverage on CRWV

IREN Appoints New Chiefs

On Wednesday, IREN revealed that it has picked Kambiz Aghili as its chief product officer. It also selected Michael Nudelman as chief development officer.

Aghili joins IREN from Oracle, where he was a vice president of products for the company's cloud business. On the other hand, Nudelman boasts more than two decades of experience in data center development, including senior roles at Google.

According to IREN, Aghili will help to develop and lead the company's product strategy and roadmap. Nudelman has also been tasked with helping IREN expand its 5-gigawatt portfolio across existing and new markets.

Why the Appointment Is Important

In the statement, IREN noted that the appointments will help to strengthen all areas of its business.

This includes its data center assets, such as substations, buildings, and cooling systems. There is also computing infrastructure, such as chips, storage, servers, and networking products. IREN also operates software tools that enable it to offer managed services to customers.

"Kambiz brings deep product leadership from one of the world's leading cloud platforms, and Michael brings a proven track record in hyperscale data center development," noted Daniel Robberts, IREN's co-founder.

What Are Analysts Saying about IREN?

The appointments come as IREN races to expand its business. It recently acquired a Spanish data center developer to enter Europe. In addition, the firm is now building a data center campus back home in Australia.

On Wall Street, analysts believe that IREN has executed "a compelling strategic pivot" from Bitcoin miner to AI cloud firm.

However, Bernstein analyst Gautam Chhugani has also argued that the company  "is behind on scale and building an enterprise business." This is compared with its neocloud rivals such as CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius (NBIS).

Is IREN a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On Wall Street, analysts remain cautious of IREN's shares and still have a Moderate Buy consensus rating. This is based on seven Buys, two Holds, and one Sell rating issued over the past three months.

However, the average IREN price target of $79.33 suggests about 108% upside in the months ahead.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why MU, SNDK, NVDA, and AMD Stocks Are Falling Today
data.description:
Shares of Micron (MU), SanDisk (SNDK), Nvidia (NVDA), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) turned lower on Thursday after opening in the green. The broader market initially moved higher following June's weaker-than-expected jobs report. However, the early rally faded as another wave of selling hit semiconductor stocks, led by sharp losses in Asian chip shares.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

200% short exposure to NVDA with NVDS

Asian Chip Stocks Led the Sell-Off

Wednesday's tech sell-off spilled over into Asia, where semiconductor stocks came under heavy pressure. South Korea's Kospi index fell nearly 8%, led by memory chip makers SK Hynix (HXSCL) and Samsung Electronics (SSNLF). SK Hynix dropped about 15%, while Samsung lost around 9%.

The weakness also spread to Hong Kong, where Semiconductor Manufacturing International (SMIC) fell about 10% and Hua Hong Semiconductor lost nearly 13%. The broad sell-off in Asian chip stocks weighed on sentiment toward U.S. semiconductor shares when trading began.

Selling Spread Across AI and Memory Stocks

The weakness was broad-based. Micron fell nearly 4%, while SanDisk dropped about 10%. Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX) each lost around 6%.

AI chip makers also came under pressure. Nvidia slipped about 1%, while AMD fell nearly 4%. Qualcomm (QCOM) and Broadcom (AVGO) also traded lower as investors continued to trim semiconductor holdings.

Investors Are Watching AI Spending Closely

Investors also remain cautious about AI spending. Technology companies have invested billions of dollars to build AI data centers over the past year. Now, the market is watching whether that spending will continue at the same pace and keep driving demand for chips and memory.

Even so, Wall Street remains bullish on the memory sector. Earlier this week, KeyBanc analyst John Vinh said tight memory supply and strong AI data center demand should continue supporting DRAM and NAND prices through 2026. He also expects higher memory prices to support earnings across the sector.

Meanwhile, China Renaissance raised its price target on SanDisk to $3,169 from $1,702, while maintaining a Buy rating. The firm expects stronger NAND pricing and growing AI storage demand to continue supporting the company's growth.

What Are the Best AI and Chip Stocks to Buy Now?

According to TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, all four stocks—Micron, SanDisk, Nvidia, and AMD—have a Strong Buy consensus rating. Analysts see the highest upside in Nvidia, with about 59%, followed by Micron at roughly 32%. SanDisk has a smaller expected upside of around 3%, while AMD's average price target suggests about 5% downside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
